highlights: Crystal-clear turquoise water with white powdery sand nestled between rugged mountains and sea
Included seal show performances at various times throughout the day for all ticket holders
Water sports available including snorkeling, kayaking, jet ski, and parachute gliding in summer

thingsToKnow.crowds: Crowded on weekends and during peak season. Early morning visits and weekday trips recommended to avoid crowds
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Requires 25km travel from Quy Nhơn city. Access only by mandatory shuttle bus or speedboat; personal motorbikes not allowed on beach
thingsToKnow.safety: Strong waves and deep water beyond shallow shore area; unsuitable for weak swimmers or young children. Swimming prohibited during off-season due to high waves
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Paid entrance fee required (120k-170k VND per adult); children 5-6 years old may not pay. Mandatory paid transport: bus/shuttle (40k-150k VND) or speedboat (10 USD). Additional charges for water activities
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Best visited November-July for swimming. Off-season (December-January) has high waves, no swimming allowed, closed facilities, and reduced services

visitorTips: Arrive early morning or weekday to avoid crowds and secure shade; bring sunscreen, sunglasses, bug spray, waterproof bag
Budget 330k+ VND total (entrance 170k + shuttle 150k-110k) or compare costs to nearby Eo Gió (22k entry) for budget options
Visit nearby Eo Gió Wind Strait cliff walkway and Hòn Kho island by speedboat tours for additional scenery and snorkeling

faq.question: When can I swim at Kỳ Co Beach?
faq.answer: Swimming is allowed during summer season only. Winter months (December-January) have strong waves and swimming is forbidden. Confirm conditions before visiting off-season.

faq.question: What are the total costs to visit?
faq.answer: Entrance fee 120k-170k VND per adult, plus mandatory shuttle/bus 40k-150k VND. Children under 5-6 may be free. Additional charges for water activities and restaurant meals.

faq.question: Is it worth visiting during off-season?
faq.answer: Off-season offers fewer crowds and no-trash beach, but swimming is prohibited, facilities closed, and high winds. Best for photography or relaxation rather than swimming.

sentiment.positive: Most visitors praised crystal-clear water, white sand, cleanliness, included seal show, and unspoiled scenery. Many called it one of Vietnam's best beaches
sentiment.negative: High entrance and transport fees viewed as tourist trap. Complaints about forced shuttle system, expensive facilities, strong seasonal waves preventing swimming, and limited off-season services
